mechanism_narrative: Sustained declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, directly boosting S&P 500 valuations over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5% within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger recession fears, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 4.5% in a single month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day forward returns in >60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a surge in consumer credit balances indicates a supply-demand mismatch where inventory drawdowns outpace spending, leading to margin compression and negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns with a directional accuracy exceeding 55% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-rate environment where the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0%, rising housing starts act as a leading indicator of resilient consumer demand, supporting earnings revisions and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Housing starts fail to exhibit a positive correlation with 20-day forward SPX returns when FEDFUNDS remains above 5.0% for consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Continuous month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects underlying economic momentum that sustains corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day trading window.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS month-over-month changes against 5-day forward SPX returns remains statistically indistinguishable from zero over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth decelerates below 1.5% annualized quarter-over-quarter, it signals an approaching economic contraction that forces downward earnings revisions and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth rate falls below 1.5%
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative when GDPC1 growth drops below the 1.5% threshold in walk-forward analysis.

mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate combined with a declining unemployment rate indicates an overheating economy where tight labor markets fuel wage-price spirals, prompting aggressive policy tightening that ultimately suppresses equity valuations over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term between FEDFUNDS changes and UNRATE changes fails to explain more than 2% of the variance in 20-day forward SPX returns in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated inflation where the CPI index exceeds 310, a sustained increase in industrial production capacity signals that supply constraints are easing, allowing profit margins to expand and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to correlate with positive 5-day forward SPX returns when CPIAUCSL remains above 310 for two consecutive months.
